audits.scope: USDe stablecoin smart contracts and core protocol
audits.summary: Comprehensive security review completed with no critical or high-level issues identified.

audits.scope: USDe stablecoin and sUSDe staking contracts
audits.summary: Full security review completed with no critical or high-level vulnerabilities identified.

audits.scope: V1 Ethena contracts including USDe and related infrastructure
audits.summary: Comprehensive audit identified 24 low/non-critical findings with no critical, high, or medium severity issues.

audits.scope: USDtb and extended Ethena ecosystem contracts
audits.summary: Full security review of USDtb stablecoin (BlackRock BUIDL-backed) with no high or medium-level findings.

audits.scope: Ethena protocol smart contracts including USDe, sENA, minting/redemption mechanisms
audits.summary: C4 analysis yielded 2 medium-severity and 5 low/non-critical issues. No critical or high-severity vulnerabilities. Medium issues: storage gap in upgradeable base contract. Mitigations reviewed and confirmed.

exploit_history.description: Fake ENA token (unrelated to Ethena Labs protocol) exploited on Binance Launchpool during airdrop announcement confusion. 480 BNB (~$290,000) lost. Caused confusion but Ethena's actual smart contracts and USDe protocol were unaffected.

Ethena Labs domain registrar account compromised, causing front-end website compromise. Domain was taken offline. Protocol contracts and user funds remained unaffected. Blockaid advised users who connected during compromise window to avoid transaction signing.

Negative funding rate stress event: USDe briefly wobbled off peg to $0.995 during market stress (Iran-Israel tensions, severe negative funding rates). USDe held peg and passed stress test but highlighted funding rate risk dependency on CEX liquidity and hedge venues.

code_health.upgrade_mechanism: Proxy pattern (likely OpenZeppelin UUPS or Transparent Proxy) with gnosis safe multisig ownership managing implementation upgrades

dependencies.notes: Institutional-grade custodian holding USDe collateral off-exchange via Off-Exchange Settlement (OES). Custody concentration risk: if Copper fails/freezes, USDe peg breaks. Monthly attestation reports published.
OES provider holding portion of USDe backing assets. Ceffu is Binance's institutional custody solution. Concentration risk: custodian failure/freeze would impact USDe.
Institutional-grade OES custodian managing USDe collateral. Multi-custodian approach mitigates single-custodian failure but maintains dependency on off-exchange settlement infrastructure.

Primary CEX for ETH perpetual hedging and funding rate execution. USDe delta-hedging model relies on Binance funding rate availability. CEX regulatory risk or trading halt would impact protocol stability.
Secondary hedge venue for ETH perp positions. Concentration of hedging across limited CEXes introduces liquidity and counterparty risk.
Tertiary ETH perpetual derivatives venue for hedging. Less liquid than Binance/OKX, increasing execution risk during high volumes.
Options market venue for optional hedging strategies. Lower operational dependency than perp venues.
Primary oracle for ETH/USD and USDe/USD price feeds. Decentralized aggregation reduces single-point-of-failure risk. Industry-standard for Ethereum stablecoins.
Secondary oracle for staked ETH price feeds. Cross-oracle dependency reduces oracle manipulation risk.
USDe backed by staked ETH and ETH delta-hedged perpetuals. ETH protocol security is industry-leading; stETH concentration risk to Lido (~30% Ethereum staking share).
USDe.sol extends ERC20Burnable, ERC20Permit, Ownable2Step from OZ audited libraries. Dependency on battle-tested standard implementations.

USDe sustainability depends on sustained positive or near-zero funding rates on perpetual hedges. Extended -50% APR rates exceed reserve fund capacity. April 2024 stress test showed USDe can briefly de-peg if funding turns extremely negative. Reserve fund adequacy depends on USDe market cap staying below $4B under worst-case negative rates.

rationale: Ethena's USDe stablecoin achieved strong security posture across 2024 with 5 major audits (Pashov, Quantstamp, Spearbit/Cantina, Cyfrin, Code4rena) producing zero critical/high-severity smart contract findings. The March 2024 fake-token exploit was unrelated to protocol; September 2024 domain compromise affected front-end only. The primary systemic risk is not in code but in operational dependencies: (1) Collateral custody concentration with Copper/Ceffu/Fireblocks creates single-point-of-failure if OES providers fail/freeze, breaking the peg; (2) Hedging venue concentration (Binance, OKX, Bybit, Deribit) introduces regulatory and liquidity risk; (3) Persistent negative funding rates on perp hedges can exhaust the reserve fund if USDe TVL exceeds $4B. The April 2024 de-peg to $0.995 during market stress highlighted this dynamic. Code health remains healthy with active GitHub development and 7/10 multisig governance. Bug bounty program ($3M max, Immunefi) is top-tier.
